title: H2Brazil Commits $1.53 Billion to Uberaba and AÃ§u Green Hydrogen Projects in Major National Push
content:
H2Brazil is investing over â‚¬1.3 billion in two green hydrogen projects in Brazil, aiming to position the country as a global leader in sustainable hydrogen and ammonia production. The flagship project in Uberaba (Minas Gerais) will feature 820MW of electrolyzer capacity, producing 125,000 tonnes of hydrogen and 700,000 tonnes of green ammonia annually. Located in an export processing zone, the facility will leverage Brazil's abundant solar and wind energy, agribusiness base, and rail logistics. The plant will occupy 70 hectares, create 1,000 direct jobs, and begin operations by 2027, with power sourced through a forthcoming agreement with Cemig, the Minas Gerais utility.

A second pilot project is planned at AÃ§u Port (Rio de Janeiro), with 15â€“20MW capacity and a budget of â‚¬40â€“50 million, also targeting 2027 for startup. H2Brazil highlights Brazil's nearly 100% green power grid, energy stability, and low PPA costs as key advantages over Europe. Estimated green hydrogen production costs in Brazil are â‚¬2.50/kg, nearly half of Europe's, despite subsidies. These projects were formalized during the World Hydrogen Summit in Rotterdam and signal growing international recognition of Brazilâ€™s hydrogen potential. H2Brazilâ€™s leadership sees these initiatives as catalysts for decarbonizing key sectors like fertilizers, methanol, and clean fuels.
author: Analysts Account
